name: Niflheim Extended
description: Niflheim, in Norse cosmology, is the primordial realm of ice, cold, and darkness. It is one of the two original realms, the other being Muspelheim, the realm of fire. Niflheim is located to the north and is characterized by perpetual winter and icy rivers. The most significant feature of Niflheim is the well Hvergelmir, from which all the rivers of the world originate. These rivers, known as the Élivágar, flow with venomous ice and create the conditions necessary for the creation of the giant Ymir. As the cold of Niflheim met the heat of Muspelheim in Ginnungagap, the void between them, the melting ice formed Ymir and the cow Auðumbla. Niflheim is also considered the home of the dragon Níðhöggr, who gnaws at the roots of Yggdrasil, the World Tree. It is a place of origins, death, and the potential for new creation, embodying the cyclical nature of the Norse universe. In some interpretations, Niflheim is associated with Hel, the realm of the dead, further cementing its connection to the end of life and the unknown. Its icy wastes and primordial nature make it a crucial element in the Norse creation myth and the overall understanding of the cosmos.
subtitle: Primordial Realm of Ice and Darkness

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Ymir
keyMyths.description: The meeting of fire from Muspelheim and ice from Niflheim in Ginnungagap resulted in the melting of ice, forming the giant Ymir and the cow Auðumbla. This marked the beginning of life in the Norse cosmos.

keyMyths.title: Níðhöggr and Yggdrasil
keyMyths.description: The dragon Níðhöggr resides in Niflheim and constantly gnaws at the roots of Yggdrasil, the World Tree. This act symbolizes the continuous cycle of destruction and renewal in the Norse universe.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, providing detailed accounts of Niflheim and its role in creation.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, providing detailed accounts of Niflheim and its role in creation.

sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that offer insights into Norse cosmology, including aspects of Niflheim.
